content.text:
The Windows XP default background image is called **"Bliss."**

**Where it was taken:** The photograph shows rolling green hills under a blue sky with white clouds. It was taken in the **Sonoma County wine region of California**, near the area around Sonoma Valley.

**Who took it:** The photo was taken by **Charles O'Rear**, a former *National Geographic* photographer. He captured the image in **1996** while driving through the area. Notably, the photograph was taken on actual film (not digitally) and reportedly received minimal digital editing.

"Bliss" became one of the most viewed photographs in the world due to Windows XP's massive global distribution after the operating system's release in 2001.
